question: Will there be 500 or more Notifiable Data Breaches reported in Australia between 1 July and 31 December 2025?
textDescription:
The Office of the Australian Information Commissioner (OAIC) publishes reports of Notifiable Data Breaches twice a year at <https://www.oaic.gov.au/privacy/notifiable-data-breaches/notifiable-data-breaches-publications>. These could be published up to five months after the period covered in the report.

Australian government agencies and large organisations must report data breaches that meet the criteria at <https://www.oaic.gov.au/privacy/notifiable-data-breaches/report-a-data-breach>

Resolves YES if the number of reported breaches is 500 or more, NO if fewer than 500. Resolves N/A if the OAIC does not publish a report for this period.
newsShortReason: Recent data indicates a consistent increase in reported data breaches in Australia.
newsKeyEvidence: OAIC's Notifiable Data Breaches Report for July to December 2024 recorded a significant number of breaches. | The OAIC launched a new dashboard in November 2025 highlighting ongoing challenges in data breach reporting. | Malicious or criminal attacks accounted for 69% of all notifications in the July to December 2024 period. | The OAIC's Notifiable Data Breach statistics dashboard presents key statistics on data breach notifications received by the OAIC under the NDB scheme since the scheme’s commencement in February 2018. | The OAIC launched a new dashboard in November 2025 highlighting ongoing challenges in data breach reporting.
